Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(11 comments)

I'm assuming this is still in the debugging stage due to LBUG()?

The per-thread cache will likely help performance, though it won't help in cases like repeated lookup of the same object for writes, or parent directories used to create lots of files.  Have you thought about a larger global cache (hashed by FID)?

Patch Set 6:

(2 comments)

> The per-thread cache will likely help performance, though it won't help in cases like repeated lookup of the same object for writes, or parent directories used to create lots of files. Have you thought about a larger global cache (hashed by FID)?

tor the cases like parents we already have LU cache (hashed by FID), though it'd be interesting to think about something like per-cpu BH cache used in the kernel

commit.subject: LU-7910 osd: do not lookup child objects in osd_dir_insert()
commit.message:
LU-7910 osd: do not lookup child objects in osd_dir_insert()

instead cache FID->dnode mapping in @env at declarations.
